name: cloudflare-ddns
description: Cloudflare Worker that acts as a DDNS provider for Synology NAS and other clients.

scripts.migrate:remote: pnpm run prepare:deploy-config:require-d1-id && wrangler d1 migrations apply DB --remote
scripts.dev: pnpm run prepare:deploy-config && wrangler d1 migrations apply DB --local && wrangler dev
scripts.test: pnpm run prepare:deploy-config && wrangler deploy --dry-run && npx vitest run --config tests/vitest.config.mts

CF_API_TOKEN.description: Cloudflare API token with Zone > DNS > Edit permission for the zone this worker will update. In the Cloudflare dashboard, start from the Edit Zone DNS template, which grants zone-scoped DNS Write access.

CF_ZONE_ID.description: Zone ID from your domain Overview page in the Cloudflare dashboard. Cloudflare shows it in the API section on the right side.

DDNS_SHARED_SECRET.description: Long random password used by Synology or other clients to authenticate update requests. Use at least 32 random characters.

DDNS_ALLOWED_HOSTNAMES.description: Comma-separated allowlist of DNS names this worker may update, for example `nas.example.com,*.nas.example.com`.

DDNS_PROXIED.description: Set to false for most NAS setups. Use true only if you want the updated records proxied through Cloudflare.

DDNS_TTL.description: Use 1 for automatic TTL, or a value from 60 to 86400 seconds.

DDNS_LOG_RETENTION_DAYS.description: How many days of update logs to keep in D1 before scheduled cleanup removes them.
